# Chronicle

Chronicle is an AI-powered CLI that turns uncommitted changes into atomic git commits with realistic timestamps.

## Overview

- Analyze working tree changes and generate commit plans
- Backfill history across a date range with realistic commit timing
- Support multiple AI providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, OpenRouter, Ollama, Cloudflare, OpenCode Zen, and Groq
- Keep binary assets out of LLM context while still handling them during commit generation

## Installation

```bash
bun i -g @atybdot/chronicle
```

## Commands

### analyze

Analyze uncommitted changes and generate a commit plan.

- `--path` - Path to git repository
- `--date-range` - Date range for commit distribution
- `--include-staged` - Include staged changes
- `--include-unstaged` - Include unstaged changes
- `--include-untracked` - Include untracked files

### backfill

Backfill git history with intelligent commit splitting.

- `--path` - Path to git repository
- `--date-range` - Date range (for example, `spread over 2 weeks`)
- `--start-date` - Start date (ISO format)
- `--end-date` - End date (ISO format)
- `--dry-run` - Preview without making changes
- `--interactive` - Interactive mode with prompts
- `--output` - Output format: `visual`, `json`, `minimal`

If git hooks fail during backfill, Chronicle can retry the current and remaining commits without hooks using `--no-verify`.

### status

Show status of current repository changes.

- `--path` - Path to git repository

### config

Configure Chronicle settings.

- `init` - Interactive setup wizard
- `show` - Show current configuration
- `model` - Change the selected model
- `provider` - Change the selected provider
- `prompt` - Set or clear custom AI instructions
- `telemetry` - Manage telemetry settings

## Configuration

Chronicle uses a multi-provider config shape.

- `llm.selected.provider` - active provider
- `llm.selected.model` - active model
- `llm.providers[]` - saved provider credentials and provider-specific settings
- Saved credentials are reused when you switch providers
- Legacy single-provider config is auto-migrated into the current shape

## AI Input Handling

- `analyze` and `backfill` are asset-aware
- Code and other text-like files are sent to your configured AI provider
- Binary files and assets stay out of LLM context
- Assets are attached to related commits when possible, or committed through deterministic fallback commits
- For models that do not support `json_schema`, Chronicle falls back to prompt-based structured output and validates the result locally
